action.text: MT. MOON -  Travelers beware — Zubat swarm in the darkness and attack without warning. Carry plenty of Poké Balls if you plan to explore the deeper tunnels.
Pressing deeper into the dark, are you? Not without a light you're not — these tunnels have swallowed better-prepared trainers than you.
Take my spare Lantern. Keep it in your bag and it'll light the way wherever you wander. Now you'll actually SEE those forks I keep going on about — always go east!
That Lantern lighting your way? Good. Stick to the main path, mind the Zubat, and if you see a fork — always go east.
Well hey there, fresh face! Name's Mac. Been mining these caves since before you were born. You look like you could use a pickaxe. Here, take my old one — it's got character.
See those sparkly rocks in the cave walls? Mine 'em with that pickaxe. Bring me what you dig up — copper, iron, silver, gold — and I'll trade you geodes in return. Fair deal, yeah?
Five Copper Chunks — you've been busy! Good honest work that. Here's a Common Geode and a little coin for your trouble.
Crack that geode open at a crusher — might be gems or shards inside. Come back when you've got more ore!
Five Iron Chunks! Now you're getting into the good stuff. Iron's the backbone of any real miner's haul. Here's an Uncommon Geode — better odds of finding something valuable inside.
Silver! Now we're talking. Three chunks of this beautiful metal will fetch you a Rare Geode. These almost always have something valuable inside. Don't spend that coin all in one place!
GOLD! In all my thirty years down here, I never get tired of seeing that shine. Two chunks gets you an Epic Geode — these are the real deal. You've got a miner's nose, kid. I mean it.
You've cleaned me out, kid! Every tier, every geode. You're a natural-born miner — best I've seen in decades. If you ever make it to deeper caves, look me up. I might have rarer trades then.
Good work so far. Keep swinging that pickaxe — the deeper you go in these caves, the better the ore gets. Come back when you've collected more.
You still owe me some ore, rookie! Swing that pickaxe at any sparkly rock you see in the cave. Copper, iron, silver, gold — bring it all to me and I'll make it worth your while.
Oh! A trainer! Perfect timing — I'm Daniella, Pokemon field researcher. I've been camped in this cave for two weeks studying Clefairy migration patterns.
Thank you! It's Kit 7B — the clerk will know. The Pewter PokeCenter is just south of the cave, past Route 3. I'll be here when you get back. ...Hopefully.
You're back! And you brought the kit — thank you! This has everything I need for the mineral analysis.
Daisy confirmed it — waxing crescent, just like I theorized! The migration routes are LUNAR, not mineral! This changes everything!

That Moon Stone works on Nidorina, Nidorino, Clefairy, Jigglypuff, and Skitty. It induces a permanent evolution — choose wisely! And if you're ever in Saffron City, I lecture at the university there. Come say hello.
Did you find the Viridian Forest Bug Catcher yet? He's got the Clefairy observation data I need. Once I have his notes, I can send them to Daisy in Pallet Town for cross-referencing.
Any luck getting my kit? Head to the Pewter City PokeCenter — they have my supplies behind the counter. Just ask for Daniella's Kit 7B. I'll be here analyzing these mineral samples.
The research continues! Thanks to you, Daisy and I are co-authoring a paper on lunar Clefairy migration.
When it's published, your name will be in the acknowledgements. Twenty years I've been chasing this theory — you helped prove it in a week.
Come find me if you're ever in Saffron — I lecture at the university there.
They're drawn to Mt. Moon's unique geology, but I can't figure out WHY. I need my cave survey kit from the Pewter City PokeCenter — they hold my research supplies behind the counter.
Ask for Daniella's Kit 7B.Please — I can't leave the dig site or the Zubat will scatter my markers! Will you go?
Here's some coin for the trouble. But there's another problem — my data is incomplete.
I need Clefairy behavioral data from a DIFFERENT location to compare with what I've gathered in this cave.
There's a Bug Catcher in Viridian Forest who caught a Clefairy recently. He's been observing it for weeks. Get his notes for me and I can finish my research.

Twenty years of cave research, finally making sense.[pauses, looks at you]You traveled from Mt. Moon to Pewter to Viridian Forest to Pallet and back to this cave — all to help a researcher you just met.
That's not something I'll forget.Here — take this Moon Stone. I've collected dozens of fragments down here, but this one is whole. Use it well. And this is for your time.
